draft.introduction: John 6:35–71 is the Bread of Life discourse, where Jesus identifies himself with the manna from heaven and declares that eternal life comes through consuming his flesh and blood. It is the most eucharistic passage in the Fourth Gospel.

translation.text: Jesus said to them, ‘I am the bread of life; whoever comes to me shall not hunger, and whoever believes in me shall never thirst. For I have come down from heaven, not to do my own will but the will of him who sent me.’
translation.original_text: εἶπεν αὐτοῖς ὁ Ἰησοῦς · ἐγώ εἰμι ὁ ἄρτος τῆς ζωῆς

translator_notes.note: The “I am” (ἐγώ εἰμι) formula echoes the divine self-revelation in Exodus 3:14. John uses this construction seven times with predicates, each making an ontological claim that links Jesus to the divine name.
